    Find A Grant
    Global Search Dialog
      1. Insights
      2. Our Research, Evaluation, and Learning

      Guaranteed Income: An Unconditional Investment in Children and Families

      Research and recommendations for evidence-based policy solutions to advance economic inclusion through guaranteed income
      Brief Apr-14-2025 Robert Wood Johnson Foundation 1-min read
      Download Roundup

      This research roundup highlights evidence from programs that provide unconditional, recurring payments and policy recommendations to scale and sustain guaranteed income. 

       

      Everyone deserves the opportunity to thrive, with access to stable housing, good jobs, and quality healthcare, free from the threat of financial hardship. Economic security is about more than money—it’s fundamental to our health and wellbeing. 

      When communities have the resources and support they need, families can spend time together, invest in their futures, and contribute to a stronger economy for all. Yet too many people are forced to make impossible choices like delaying medical care to afford rent, or spending less time with family to work an extra job to put food on the table.

      Guaranteed income—an unconditional and recurring payment provided directly to people and families to supplement their existing resources—is one pathway to address these inequities and achieve economic inclusion for every family, regardless of background or ZIP code.

       

      Key Findings

      Since 2017, more than 150 guaranteed income pilots and programs have launched across the United States. The growing body of research on these initiatives continues to show that guaranteed income:

      • Increases financial stability by helping families pay bills on time, build savings, and reduce debt
      • Improves physical, mental, and emotional health by helping families afford healthcare, medical necessities like eyeglasses, and nutritious foods and by reducing stress, anxiety, and depression
      • Enhances family wellbeing by giving parents and caregivers more time with their children
      • Creates pathways to opportunity by enabling recipients to pursue education, job training, and full-time employment
      • Provides autonomy and dignity by allowing families to make their own decisions about how to meet their unique needs

       

      Policy Recommendations

      To scale and sustain guaranteed income nationwide, policies like the Child Tax Credit and Earned Income Tax Credit can be reimagined as forms of guaranteed income. When designed equitably—by making them fully refundable, removing work requirements, distributing payments monthly, and ensuring all families have access—these tax credits can provide millions of families with the recurring, unconditional financial support they need to thrive.

      Related Content

      Economic Inclusion for Family Wellbeing

      Every family should have the opportunity to make healthy choices, and we're here to help establish the building blocks for good health across our nation.

      Child and Family Wellbeing
      Library

      Work Requirements

      We work to ensure that all parents and caregivers have the resources they need to promote thriving families. 

      Healthcare Coverage and Access Policy and Law
      Library

      Premium Tax Credits

      The enhanced premium tax credits were put in place by Congress during COVID-19 to help people maintain their health coverage and are set to expire at the end of 2025.

      This research highlights evidence-based policy solutions and recommendations.

      Are you an RWJF Grantee (Past or Present?)
      Are you a European Union Resident?
      Tell us what type of content you want to receive. Select at least one (required).

      Be informed with our twice a month newsletter updating you with relevant news and research around a Culture of Health, as well as the latest funding opportunities.

      Get funded by RWJF: Receive notifications when new funding opportunities are released.

      Receive monthly updates on RWJF-sponsored research that informs many robust health policy debates on Capitol Hill, covering topics like health equity, improving access to quality healthcare, equitable housing, and more.

      Shop talk for researchers. This monthly newsletter covers research news and opportunities from the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation.

      Communications expedite action. Get periodic research and expert insights on the best ways to communicate so we can spread what works.

      Sometimes we have news, announcements or opportunities that don't quite fit the subscription parameters above. If you're interested, we'll send you this information under "There's more...". *If you've indicated you are an EU resident, we will only send these communications if you intentionally check this box.
